results.title: A Merry Chase: Prelude a L'apres midi d'un faune by Claude Debussy
results.brief_description: Excerpt from Bruno Bozzetto’s “Allegro Non Troppo”. A squat and aging satyr lusts for the sexual attention of young nymphs of the serene woodland. Music from Debussy.

results.title: Let it bee: Concerto in C Minor by Vivaldi
results.brief_description: Excerpt from Bruno Bozzetto’s “Allegro Non Troppo”. A domesticated honey-bee prepares a picnic with wine and cheese but is disturbed by a romancing couple bent also on picnicking. Music from Vivaldi. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: The Last meow: 'Valse Triste', Jean Sibelius
results.brief_description: Excerpt from “Allegro Non Troppo” by Bruno Bozzetto with music by Jean Sibelius. A scrawny cat prowls the crumbling ruins of a house, remembering the warmth and snugness of family life he once enjoyed. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: From one to many: Dvorak's 'Slavic Dance No. 7'
results.brief_description: Excerpt from Bruno Bozzetto’s “Allegro Non Troppo”. An animated comment on social confirmity set to the music of Dvorak. Featuring the Berlin Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Herbert Von Karajan. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Evolutionary fantasy: Bolero by Maurice Ravel from Allegro n
results.creator_credit: Bruno Bozzetto and Bruno Bozzetto Films
results.brief_description: In this animated segment from the feature film “Allegro non troppo”. Ravel’s “Bolero” accompanies a satiric fantasy of the evolution of life beginning with an amoebic blob emerging from a coke bottle.

results.title: The Romans: life, laughter and laws
results.creator_credit: Ian Brims, John H. Secondari and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Examines certain facets of the Roman way of life: dictatorship; Julius Caesar; poetry; Imperial Rome; law; and the evils of living in an urban society. Suitable for lower secondary level.

results.title: Interviews with My Lai veterans
results.creator_credit: Joseph Strick and Laser Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Five American soldiers present their recollections of the events at My Lai, Vietnam on 16th March 1968. During their interviews such matters as the training of soldiers, the strategy of My Lai and the details of the massacre are discussed. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Universe and I: Luna the lovely
results.brief_description: An introduction to concepts derived from the theory of relativity, emphasising the relationships between the dimensions of time and space, matter and energy, and set in the context of a science fiction filmmaker researching these issues. Suitable for lower and middle secondary levels.

results.title: Mind-slaughter
results.brief_description: A look at the atmosphere of the earth and other planets as oceans of gases providing planetary life-support systems, and the consequences of an experiment to create an atmosphere for human habitation on another planet at the cost of existing life forms. Dramatically presented but fascinating comparative analysis, nonetheless, of earth and venus’s atmosphere - with a view to human habitation on the latter. Suitable for lower and middle secondary levels.

results.title: Track stars: the unseen heroes of movie sound
results.creator_credit: Terry Burke, Film Arts and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Split-screen technique is used to demonstrate how “track stars” - movie sound specialists - painstakingly match sounds to the action of the characters who are being filmed.
